pubmed_links: [Shoichet et al. (2017) Mapping autosomal recessive intellectual disability: combined microarray and exome sequencing identifies 26 novel candidate genes](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/28397838/); [Coffey et al. (2014) Nuclear and cytosolic JNK signalling in neurons](https://pubmed.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/25309320/); [Bhatt et al. (2018) JIP1-mediated JNK activation negatively regulates synaptic plasticity and spatial memory](https://pmc.ncbi.nlm.nih.gov/articles/PMC5895995/)
summary: MAPK8 (JNK1) encodes a kinase whose signaling pathway has been genetically implicated in autism spectrum disorder, schizophrenia, and intellectual disability; loss-of-function variants in MAPK8 were identified in consanguineous families with autosomal recessive intellectual disability, and JNK1 dysregulation disrupts synaptic plasticity and dendritic architecture relevant to neurodevelopmental phenotypes. (emerging evidence)
